title: T1000-E CoAP round trip does not complete despite working RX + gateway acceptance
description:
The T1000-E (LR1110) does not complete a CoAP GET /status round trip even though every prerequisite is verified working (2026-07-18, all observed over SMP if02 - the board has no console):
- LR1110 RX works: 'lr1110' STATS rxdone climbs steadily with valid frames (qpc0 resolved).
- Gateway accepts the T1000-E's GETs: with 8a1a2c4b93812cab pinned, only 1 auth-fail in 50s; its frames verify.
- Not airtime alone: the T-Echo still completes 2/6 (33%) round trips under the SAME 3-node contention, while the T1000-E gets 'coap' STATS ok=0/9 over 80s (all err/timeout).
So it is T1000-E-specific. RX demodulates frames but the gateway's response to the T1000-E is never matched to its GET.

Leads to chase (each needs a small instrumentation + OTA cycle):
1. Add an L2-level rx-delivered-to-app counter (frames that pass peer verify AND are addressed to us AND injected to the IPv6 stack) on the T1000-E, read over SMP. If 0 while rxdone>0, the gateway's response never reaches L2-for-us (routing/addressing/replay); if >0 while coap.ok=0, the gap is above L2 (CoAP token match / request already timed out).
2. Gateway-side: log/count CoAP responses actually TXed to the T1000-E's address (is the CoAP server responding + routing to it, or dropping under 3-node load?).
3. Suspect the T1000-E lacks epoch persistence (only T-Echo + gateway have CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_EPOCH_PERSIST) - check whether the T1000-E's replay window rejects the gateway's response, though its fresh-boot window should accept. 
4. TX->RX turnaround: does the LR1110 re-arm RX fast enough after the T1000-E's own GET TX to catch a prompt response?

The lr1110 + coap STATS groups and the gateway 2-peer pin are already in place to support this. Reference: session 011WNEwnjQpVRfApJFak8Cpd.
close_reason: FIXED 2026-07-18 by an RX1-style gateway response delay. Root cause (proven, prior comments): the T1000-E's half-duplex LR1110 was still in TX/turnaround when the gateway's <1ms-latency reply to its own GET arrived, so it missed its own responses (received none in the isolation test) while overhearing responses to other nodes. Fix: CONFIG_LICHEN_GATEWAY_RX1_DELAY_MS - the gateway waits before answering a CoAP request (set to 100ms on the Heltec bench gateway), like LoRaWAN Class-A RX1, giving the requester time to switch TX->RX. Result: T1000-E coap.ok went from a PERMANENT 0 (0/639 all session) to actively climbing (0->2 in an 80s window, ~50% of GETs in that window) - the T1000-E now completes full CoAP round trips. The T-Echo (SX1262, fast turnaround) is unaffected: 2/6 with and without the delay. All three nodes now mesh end-to-end. Removed the ad-hoc lichen_l2 TX-dest INF log (diagnostic only); kept the gateway status_get GET/respond log + the lr1110/l2rx/coap STATS groups as permanent observability. Remaining rate is bounded by 3-node SF10 airtime contention, not this bug.
